market_summary: REGIME: RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE SUSTAINED — SPY close=655.24, 1m=-3.42%, 3m=-4.36%, below BOTH MA50 and MA200 for 5th+ consecutive session; QQQ 1m=-2.75%, 3m=-5.55%, also below both MAs. thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance cadence minimum spacing = 7 days; last checkpoint = 2026-04-01; next eligible window = ~2026-04-08. Policy mandates do-nothing. Portfolio is well-positioned for the April 2 tariff binary: Energy (XOM+CVX=42%, both above MA50+MA200), Commodities (GLD+SLV=24%, GLD buffer=+15.7% above MA200), Defense (LMT=10%), and Consumer Staples (COST=6%) collectively represent 82% of portfolio in the most tariff-resilient sectors. Technology (KLAC+TSM=18%) carries binary tariff risk today but both retain positive 3m momentum and above-MA200 status.

FACTOR EDGE: Portfolio weighted momentum composite ~+29% vs SPY=+2.40% — +27pp alpha spread is the widest maintained across this run series. All 8 holdings have positive momentum composites. KLAC EPS ramp Q2=$9.74→Q4=$12.10 is the strongest confirmed fundamental acceleration in the universe. XOM/CVX Q2-Q3 EPS trajectories ($2.35-$2.49 / $2.46-$2.53) confirm energy thesis with durable free cash flow. COST EPS acceleration to $6.43 in Q4-2026 provides defensive quality anchor. GLD MA200 buffer recovered to +15.7% from crisis-level +6.5% on 2026-03-31 — structural inflation hedge re-established.

VARIANT VIEW: The market is pricing in severe tariff shock across all sectors indiscriminately. This portfolio's concentrated Energy/Commodities/Defense/Consumer Staples mix is structurally positioned to outperform in a persistent tariff-driven risk-off regime. If April 2 tariff announcement is milder than feared (the 'bark worse than bite' scenario), the energy/commodity overweight may lag briefly but the momentum factors across all 8 holdings should re-accelerate on any relief rally.
risks: MACRO: April 2 'Liberation Day' tariff announcement could trigger violent gap moves in TSM (-geopolitical exposure to Taiwan chip tariffs) and KLAC (-semiconductor equipment export controls). A severe blanket tariff regime could compress oil demand expectations and pressure XOM/CVX despite domestic production advantages. GLD could sell off on tariff-induced USD strength or forced liquidation. SPY below MA200 for 5+ sessions signals systemic regime deterioration that could overwhelm sector-relative positioning.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: LMT 1m=-7.51% acceleration below MA50 is the most urgent monitoring flag — if close below MA50 persists for 3+ sessions, defense budget cut risk or program cancellation news must be evaluated. SLV dd63=-42.45% with vol20=58.55% means a 2-sigma adverse day could breach MA200 emergency floor ($52.68) despite current buffer. TSM tariff binary today is the highest-probability single-day gap risk in the portfolio.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: Non-rebalance day — no execution planned. All holdings are highly liquid. Next rebalance window (~2026-04-08) should evaluate: (1) TSM sizing if tariff outcome is severe; (2) LMT MA50 recovery/failure; (3) Whether SLV 2% floor remains optimal vs elimination; (4) Whether KLAC continued above-both-MAs strength warrants size increase from 10%.
falsifiable_checks: Portfolio alpha thesis is validated if weighted portfolio momentum spread vs SPY remains >+20pp at next rebalance checkpoint (2026-04-08); if spread compresses below +15pp with SPY recovering above MA200, regime is shifting and defensive overweight requires re-evaluation.

TSM tariff kill-switch: if TSM closes below MA200 ($288.31) on or before 2026-04-08, immediate replacement evaluation is triggered (candidates: CVX size increase or LLY if 3m momentum recovers above 0%). LMT kill-switch: if LMT closes below MA200 ($507.41) before 2026-04-08, exit and reallocate to XOM or GLD.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case (70%): April 2 tariff announcement is within expected parameters; portfolio's Energy/Commodities/Defense/Consumer Staples concentration continues to outperform on relative basis. The do-nothing discipline today maximizes cost efficiency — no turnover drag on a non-rebalance day where the portfolio already has optimal defensive positioning. Bull case (20%): Tariff announcement is milder than feared; energy and commodities lead a relief rally while KLAC/TSM recover toward MA50. KLAC's EPS ramp ($9.74→$12.10) provides a powerful re-rating catalyst in this scenario. Bear case (10%): Severe universal tariff shock triggers broad liquidation; XOM/CVX benefit from energy security premium but KLAC/TSM face export-control compounding risk. In this scenario, the 24% GLD allocation provides portfolio ballast (12m=+51.95%, MA200 buffer=+15.7%). Expected edge of doing nothing today: zero turnover cost vs. 20-30bps round-trip slippage on any trade; portfolio is already optimally positioned for the binary outcome.
